summary: Stunning audience tape with razor‑sharp stereo imaging captures a stellar show highlighted by a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s Tower and Terrapin.
review: The recording is an exceptionally clear audience master – the two‑mic setup delivers vivid left/right separation (vocals on the left, Jerry on the right) and every instrument sits nicely in the mix. The performance itself is a fan favorite, with a blazing second set featuring Help > Slip > Franklin’s Tower, Terrapin Station, Mexicali Blues and a soulful Samson, only marred by a weak Touch‑of‑Grey outing.

ai_rating.rationale: All 12 reviews are overwhelmingly positive (mostly 5 stars) and praise both the superb audience recording quality and the high‑energy show; only one song is criticized, so the combined rating is near the top of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Two Sony cardioid mics give excellent stereo imaging (vocals left, Jerry right), clear instrument separation and a warm analog sound; no major drop‑outs or noise reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A powerhouse second set that lifts the entire concert; the only dip is a lackluster Touch‑of‑Grey, but overall the band rides a hot, relaxed vibe throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia's tone is described as 'over the top' and his guitar work is highlighted for its brilliance and vivid placement in the mix.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s slide chords on Samson are praised as cruelly effective, adding to the set’s excitement.
